title: Format a Score with f-Strings
description: Create variables `name = 'Alice'` and `score = 87.5`. Then use an **f-string with a format specifier** to print exactly: `Alice scored 87.50%`. The score must show exactly 2 decimal places (`.2f`) followed by a percent sign `%`.
starter_code:
name = 'Alice'
score = 87.5

# Use an f-string to print the formatted result

code_requirements.description: Uses an f-string (starts with f followed by a quote)
code_requirements.fail_message: Use an f-string prefixed with 'f' like: f'...' or f"..."

code_requirements.description: Uses a format specifier with 'f' for float formatting
code_requirements.fail_message: Add a format specifier like :.2f inside curly braces to show the score with 2 decimal places.

hints: Your f-string should look like: f'...'
Use {score:.2f} inside the f-string to show the score with 2 decimal places
The final output should be: Alice scored 87.50% — note the percent sign is a literal character, not part of the format specifier
